# HeptaSieve

> Privacy-first, AI-safe, continuous local sync from Heptabase to structured Markdown on macOS. A fail-closed allow-list decides exactly which Heptabase cards are exported to AI-readable Markdown; everything else is never read. The point of the tool is to keep confidential cards out of an AI agent's reach while exporting only the cards you allow.

Key facts an agent should hold while working with this project:
- It reads a local SQLite database (`hepta.db`) maintained by the Heptabase desktop app, read-only.
- A card is exported only if it matches `whitelist_whiteboards` (a whiteboard you named) or `card_map` (a title override). `blacklist_whiteboards` subtracts cards before any content is read and wins over the whitelist.
- An AI agent must read only the exported Markdown folder, never `hepta.db`. That separation is the whole privacy model.
- Real configuration lives in `config.json` (gitignored). The committed example is `config.example.json`.

## Docs

- [README](README.md): what it is, why it exists, the fail-closed model, install and setup.
- [Architecture](ARCHITECTURE.md): data flow, the fail-closed order inside build_plan, the database tables read, and the privacy invariants to preserve when modifying code.
- [Agent guide](AGENTS.md): how an AI agent should configure, run, and reason about this tool.
- [Example config](config.example.json): every field documented inline.
